title: Leek-y Gnocchi Traybake
summary: Creamy, tangy, and packed with greens, this one-tray wonder is a weeknight hero. Roasting the leeks brings out their natural sweetness, while Dijon and crème fraîche add just the right amount of zing.

method.description: Prep the veg. Trim and discard the tough outer layer of the leeks, then cut them into 3cm chunks. Halve the Tenderstem broccoli. Peel and finely slice the garlic. Grate the Parmesan and set aside.
Make the dressing. In a small bowl, mix the crème fraîche and Dijon mustard with a good pinch of salt and pepper. Set aside.
Tip the leeks into a large roasting tray, drizzle with olive oil, scatter over the fennel seeds, and season well. Toss to coat and roast for 10 minutes.
Meanwhile, pour boiling water over the gnocchi in a large bowl. Leave to sit for 1 minute, then drain well.

Add the blanched gnocchi, sliced garlic, and Tenderstem to the tray. Pour over the vegetable stock, toss everything together, then sprinkle over the grated Parmesan. Return to the oven for 15–25 minutes, or until golden and tender.
Once out of the oven, stir through the crème fraîche and mustard mix until glossy and creamy. Taste and season with salt, pepper or a pinch of sugar, if neeed.
Pick and roughly chop the parsley, then scatter it over the tray before serving.

tips: Tenderstem broccoli → Use regular broccoli florets or asparagus
Crème fraîche → Swap for Greek yoghurt or sour cream
Parmesan → Use pecorino or a vegetarian hard cheese
Dijon mustard → Try wholegrain mustard for extra texture
Gnocchi → Cauliflower gnocchi or gluten-free gnocchi for dietary needs
Make ahead: Roast the veg and gnocchi, then store in the fridge for up to 3 days. Reheat in the oven with a splash of stock before stirring in the crème fraîche mix.
Lunches: Portion into containers and garnish with fresh parsley just before eating.

chefs.name: Jodie Nixon
chefs.summary: Jodie is a recipe writer, Mob food producer, and professional snacker. When she's not recreating all your childhood classics, she's creating delicious veg-forward dishes that'll prove you don't always need meat to have a good time. She's one hell of a talented cook.
